question: Subject: Quarterly Meeting Invitation... Please ____ your attendance by Friday so we can finalize the catering.
explain: confirm 是商務會議中常用的詞彙，表示「確認」出席。
fullSentence: Please confirm your attendance by Friday so we can finalize the catering.

question: A person whose job is to keep or inspect financial accounts.
explain: accountant 指的是「會計師」。
fullSentence: An accountant is a person whose job is to keep or inspect financial accounts.

question: The project was completed on time ____ the unexpected technical difficulties encountered during the final phase.
explain: despite (儘管) 後面接名詞或名詞短語，用來表示轉折。
fullSentence: The project was completed on time despite the unexpected technical difficulties.

question: Could you please let me know when the shipment is expected to arrive?|It should be there by tomorrow morning.|I will send it now.|The price is too high.|I am busy today.
tag: 回應關於貨物抵達時間的詢問，A 選項最符合商務邏輯。
explain: intermediate
level: It should be there by tomorrow morning.

question: We are writing to ____ you that your application has been successfully processed.
explain: inform someone that... 是正式商務書信常用的寫法。
fullSentence: We are writing to inform you that your application has been successfully processed.

question: Your application for the position of Marketing Assistant has been received and is currently under ____.
explain: under review 是常見的商務片語，表示「正在審核中」。
fullSentence: Your application for the position of Marketing Assistant has been received and is currently under review.

question: Please be advised that the new policy will be ____ on July 1st.
explain: effective on + 日期，表示「於某日生效」。
fullSentence: Please be advised that the new policy will be effective on July 1st.

question: We apologize for any inconvenience this may ____.
explain: cause inconvenience 是常見的搭配。
fullSentence: We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.

question: The updated report is attached ____ your reference.
explain: for your reference 表示「供您參考」。
fullSentence: The updated report is attached for your reference.

question: We look forward to ____ from you soon.
explain: look forward to + Ving，表示「期待做某事」。
fullSentence: We look forward to hearing from you soon.

question: A formal meeting at which someone is asked questions to see if they are suitable for a job.
explain: interview 指「面試」。
fullSentence: An interview is a formal meeting at which someone is asked questions to see if they are suitable for a job.

question: The total amount of money that a country or company has at a particular time.
explain: assets 指「資產」。
fullSentence: Assets refer to the total amount of money that a country or company has at a particular time.

question: A person who is employed to drive a private or rented car.
explain: chauffeur 指「私人司機」。
fullSentence: A chauffeur is a person who is employed to drive a private or rented car.

question: A detailed plan showing how something will be done.
explain: blueprint 指「藍圖」或「詳細計畫」。
fullSentence: A blueprint is a detailed plan showing how something will be done.

question: To officially offer someone a job.
explain: appoint 指「任命，委派」。
fullSentence: To appoint someone is to officially offer someone a job.

question: She insisted ____ going to the meeting, even though she was feeling ill.
explain: insist on 後面接動名詞，表示「堅持做某事」。
fullSentence: She insisted on going to the meeting, even though she was feeling ill.

question: ____ the sudden change in weather, the outdoor event was postponed.
explain: Due to (由於) 後面接名詞或名詞短語，表示原因。
fullSentence: Due to the sudden change in weather, the outdoor event was postponed.

question: The new software, ____ developed by our internal team, will be launched next month.
explain: which was developed... 為非限定修飾語，修飾 new software。
fullSentence: The new software, which was developed by our internal team, will be launched next month.

question: Not only ____ excellent in her field, but she is also a great mentor.
explain: Not only 開頭的句子需要倒裝。
fullSentence: Not only is she excellent in her field, but she is also a great mentor.

question: The manager asked ____ the report was finished or not.
explain: ask whether... or not... 是常用句型，表示「是否」。
fullSentence: The manager asked whether the report was finished or not.

question: I am calling to inquire about the status of my order
options: #12345. | It will be shipped by the end of the week. | We are out of stock. | You can place a new order. | I will call you back later.
explain: 針對訂單狀態的詢問，告知預計出貨時間最為恰當。
fullSentence: It will be shipped by the end of the week.

question: The printer is not working again. Do you know what the problem is? | Have you tried turning it off and on again? | You should buy a new one. | It is very old. | I am not a technician.
tag: 詢問問題時，最常見的故障排除步驟是重啟。
explain: beginner
level: Have you tried turning it off and on again?

question: We need to submit the budget proposal by 5 PM today. Will you be able to finish it on time? | I am almost done; I just need to finalize the figures. | I have not started yet. | I am too busy. | I need more time.
tag: 表達進度並承諾完成，最能讓主管安心。
explain: advanced
level: I am almost done; I just need to finalize the figures.

question: I would like to request a day off next Monday. | Let me check the schedule and get back to you. | You cannot take days off. | That is not possible. | Why do you need a day off?
tag: 檢查排程後再回覆，是標準的 HR 應對。
explain: intermediate
level: Let me check the schedule and get back to you.

question: Thank you for your assistance. I really appreciate it. | You are most welcome! | No problem. | It was nothing. | Don't mention it.
tag: You are most welcome! 是更為正式和熱情的感謝回覆。
explain: beginner
